title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first composed handler and dependency boundary for handlers, middleware, dependencies, and application architecture. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of composed handler and dependency boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Handler Interface Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 8040 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Handler Interface Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use http.handler and handlerfunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Handler Interface Contract, keep Handler Interface Contract connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
